steps.title: Use the setting effect
steps.content: The passage says lab studies are useful for controlling variables, but kestrels' surroundings can significantly affect their hormone levels. The finding should therefore show a hormone difference between kestrels in different settings.

steps.title: Keep the species and setting
steps.content: A compares wild male kestrels with captive male canaries, mixing species. B says wild and captive males had similar levels, contradicting the expected setting effect. C describes differences between individuals, not differences caused by wild versus captive settings. The finding about higher breeding-season androgen levels in wild males fits the logic. The answer is D.
